title: Hypenia micrantha
intro: Hypenia micrantha, a species of flowering plant in the Lamiaceae family, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing oxidative stress, alleviating respiratory issues, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Hypenia micrantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hypenia micrantha is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Hypenia micrantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Hypenia, which is part of the mint family. The plant is native to South America and is known for its small, fragrant flowers. Hypenia micrantha is an annual or perennial herb, with a growth habit that varies depending on the environment. Further classification of the plant is as follows: Hypenia micrantha is a member of the tribe Ocimeae, and its botanical name is often considered to be a synonym for the genus Hyptis.
